title: Integrity and Ethical Values
text: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values through its standards of conduct, tone at the top, and enforcement of accountability.

title: Board Independence and Oversight
text: The board of directors demonstrates independence from management and exercises oversight of the development and performance of internal control.

title: Organizational Structure
text: Management establishes, with board oversight, structures, reporting lines, and appropriate authorities and responsibilities in pursuit of objectives.

title: Commitment to Competence
text: The entity demonstrates a commitment to attract, develop, and retain competent individuals in alignment with objectives, including security training.

title: Accountability
text: The entity holds individuals accountable for their internal control responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives.

title: Quality Information
text: The entity obtains or generates and uses relevant, quality information to support the functioning of internal control.

title: Internal Communication
text: The entity internally communicates information, including objectives and responsibilities for internal control, necessary to support its functioning.

title: External Communication
text: The entity communicates with external parties regarding matters affecting the functioning of internal control.

title: Specifies Objectives
text: The entity specifies objectives with sufficient clarity to enable the identification and assessment of risks relating to objectives.

title: Identifies and Analyzes Risk
text: The entity identifies risks to the achievement of its objectives across the entity and analyzes risks as a basis for determining how they should be managed.

title: Considers Fraud Risk
text: The entity considers the potential for fraud in assessing risks to the achievement of objectives.

title: Identifies Changes
text: The entity identifies and assesses changes that could significantly impact the system of internal control.

title: Monitoring Activities
text: The entity selects, develops, and performs ongoing and/or separate evaluations to ascertain whether the components of internal control are present and functioning.

title: Evaluates and Communicates Deficiencies
text: The entity evaluates and communicates internal control deficiencies in a timely manner to those parties responsible for taking corrective action.

title: Selects Control Activities
text: The entity selects and develops control activities that contribute to the mitigation of risks to the achievement of objectives to acceptable levels.

title: Technology Control Activities
text: The entity selects and develops general control activities over technology to support the achievement of objectives.

title: Policies and Procedures
text: The entity deploys control activities through policies that establish what is expected and procedures that put policies into action.

title: Logical Access Security
text: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets, including authentication and MFA, to protect them from security events.

title: Registration and Authorization
text: Prior to issuing system credentials, the entity registers and authorizes new internal and external users; credentials are removed when access is no longer required.

title: Role-Based Access
text: The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access to data and systems based on roles, least privilege, and segregation of duties, with periodic access reviews.

title: Physical Access
text: The entity restricts physical access to facilities and protected information assets to authorized personnel.

title: Asset Disposal
text: The entity discontinues logical and physical protections over physical assets only after the ability to read or recover data has been diminished and is no longer required.

title: Boundary Protection
text: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.

title: Data Transmission and Movement
text: The entity restricts the transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorized users and protects it during transmission and storage through encryption.

title: Malicious Software Prevention
text: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software.

title: Vulnerability Detection
text: The entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify changes to configurations that introduce new vulnerabilities and to detect susceptibilities to newly discovered vulnerabilities.

title: Security Monitoring
text: The entity monitors system components and the operation of controls for anomalies indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors, and evaluates events for impact.

title: Incident Evaluation
text: The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they could or have resulted in a failure to meet objectives (security incidents) and responds accordingly.

title: Incident Response
text: The entity responds to identified security incidents by executing a defined incident response program to understand, contain, remediate, and communicate.

title: Recovery
text: The entity identifies, develops, and implements activities to recover from identified security incidents.

title: Change Management
text: The entity authorizes, designs, develops, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its objectives.

title: Risk Mitigation
text: The entity identifies, selects, and develops risk mitigation activities for risks arising from potential business disruptions.

title: Vendor and Business Partner Risk
text: The entity assesses and manages risks associated with vendors and business partners, including security review and ongoing monitoring.

title: Capacity Management
text: The entity maintains, monitors, and evaluates current processing capacity and use of system components to manage capacity demand and enable additional capacity.

title: Environmental Protections and Backup
text: The entity authorizes, designs, implements, and operates environmental protections, software, data backup processes, and recovery infrastructure to meet availability objectives.

title: Recovery Testing
text: The entity tests recovery plan procedures supporting system recovery to meet its availability objectives.

title: Confidential Information Identification
text: The entity identifies and maintains confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.

title: Confidential Information Disposal
text: The entity disposes of confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.

title: Processing Integrity Objectives
text: The entity obtains or generates, uses, and communicates relevant, quality information regarding the objectives related to processing integrity.

title: Input Completeness and Accuracy
text: The entity implements policies and procedures over system inputs, including controls over completeness and accuracy, to meet processing integrity objectives.

title: Processing Completeness and Accuracy
text: The entity implements policies and procedures over system processing to result in products, services, and reporting that meet the entity's objectives.
